title: Tawa Grilled Fish with Lemon

ingredients.name: firm white fish fillets (tilapia, basa or seer)

subs.name: large prawns — cook 2 min per side only
sambar powder (1 tsp) for a Tamil-style spice profile
gingelly oil for a south Indian coastal flavour

steps.text: Pat the fish fillets completely dry with a kitchen cloth — surface moisture is the enemy of a good crust. Score each fillet with 2-3 shallow diagonal cuts to help the marinade penetrate and prevent curling during cooking.

Mix lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin, black pepper and salt into a thick paste. Coat the fillets on both sides, pressing the marinade into the cuts. Rest for 10 minutes — do not marinate longer or the lemon acid will start to cook the fish.
Heat a tawa or heavy frying pan on high until smoking — this is critical for a seared crust rather than a steamed finish. Add the oil and swirl to coat the surface evenly.

Place the fillets in a single layer and press gently with a flat spatula for the first 30 seconds to ensure full contact with the surface. Sear without moving for 4-5 minutes until the underside is deeply coloured and releases cleanly from the pan.

Flip carefully using a wide spatula and cook the other side 3-4 minutes until the fish flakes when pressed at the thickest point. Serve immediately on a warm plate with lemon wedges, coriander leaves and a side salad or steamed rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.

substitution_notes: fish → large prawns — cook 2 min per side only; oil → gingelly oil for a south Indian coastal flavour; coriander-seeds → sambar powder (1 tsp) for a Tamil-style spice profile
